description:
An At-risk grading permit can be requested to start grading work prior to construction permit issuance and approval from the Civil division.  However, NO work besides grading can be done with an at-risk grading permit.  No structures, piping, trenching, etc. may be constructed under the AR permit.  Additionally, no utilities can be installed with an at-risk grading permit.

ADEQ NOI and Maricopa County Dust Control permit, and the contractor verification form are required.   
 
This project is an approximate 50.5-arcre development consisting of 1 industrial building with associated truck and vehicular parking.
source: Mesa AZ – Commercial Building Permits (JSON)

description:
add (1) 30a. 120v twist lock receptacle on an existing dedicated circuit for IT rack in electrical room for building 2
*The circuit that we want to extend is existing and is listed in the existing panel directory as IT Rack.   The circuit was routed to a junction box in the existing electrical room and the past contractor never completed the installation of the IT receptacle and abandoned the circuit in the junction box within the electrical room of building 2.  We need to extend this existing circuit about 15 via 3/4 emt conduit w/ (3) #10s and install the outlet box with a new 30amp twistlock device in it for connection of IT equipment.
source: Austin TX – Issued Construction Permits (Live API – Fixed)

description:
This project involves the remodel of an existing bathroom within an occupied structure. The total scope is confined to a clearly defined interior area and does not include work in the adjacent areas marked as NOT IN SCOPE on the provided plans.
The remodel includes interior upgrades to plumbing fixtures partitions finishes and select building systems. This project is part of a 205 sq. ft. interior renovation focused on improving functionality accessibility and compliance with applicable codes (plumbing ADA etc.)
